x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Eligibility Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies which transactions or parties qualify for a chargeback.

Comment:
Examples:
Unauthorized or fraudulent transactions
Duplicate charges
Non-delivery or defective goods/services
Transactions outside agreed terms

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Initiation Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how a customer or merchant can initiate a chargeback request.

Includes:
Required documentation (receipts, dispute forms)
Channels for submitting (online, branch, phone)
Time limits for initiation (e.g., within 60 days of transaction)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Timeframe and Deadline Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines the allowable time period for initiating and processing chargebacks.

Comment:
Examples:
Dispute must be filed within 60120 days of the transaction
Bank has 30 days to acknowledge the request
Merchant has 10 days to respond to the dispute

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Calculation Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how the amount to be reversed is calculated, including fees, interest, or adjustments.

Comment:
Examples:
Full transaction amount refunded
Partial refund for goods returned
Deduction of any applicable fees

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Supporting Documentation Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies what evidence is required to validate the chargeback.

Commnent:
Examples:
Transaction receipts
Proof of non-delivery or defective service
Signed dispute forms

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Merchant and Customer Responsibility Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines the obligations of the parties involved during the chargeback process.

Comment:
Examples:
Merchant must respond within the specified timeframe
Customer must provide evidence of dispute
Both parties must maintain communication for resolution

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Fee and Penalty Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies any fees, penalties, or interest applicable to chargebacks.

Comment:
Examples:
Chargeback fee applied to merchant
Interest accrued on reversed amounts
Penalties for late submission of dispute evidence

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Resolution Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how disputes are resolved once a chargeback request is processed.

Includes:
Full or partial reversal approval
Rejection criteria
Escalation procedures for unresolved disputes

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Regulatory and Compliance Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Ensures chargeback processes comply with laws, card network rules, and banking regulations.

Comment:
Examples:
Compliance with Visa, Mastercard, or local card network rules
AML/KYC verification
Reporting to regulators if required

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Notification Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how and when parties are notified about chargeback status.

Includes:
Acknowledgement of dispute submission
Merchant response notification
Final resolution confirmation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Chargeback Dispute Escalation and Arbitration Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how unresolved or contested chargebacks are escalated.

Comment
Examples:
Referral to card network arbitration
Internal bank escalation to management
Mediation procedures
